docs.title: StudyFinder — Product Requirements Document
docs.text:
StudyFinder is a clinical research study discovery and outreach platform that helps users find relevant studies, organize opportunities, manage sponsor and provider contacts, and conduct outreach through automated email sequences. It brings study data, contact information, scouting workflows, reporting, analytics, and outreach tools into one place so users can identify opportunities and act on them efficiently.

Status: Draft · Owner: Product · Last updated: 2026-07-02

docs.title: Foundation
docs.text:
• 1. Product Overview — what StudyFinder is and does
  • 2. Product Goals — the outcomes the product optimizes for
  • 3. Subscription Tiers & Feature Access — Lite, Core, Growth, Enterprise; features, limits, pricing, upgrade paths

docs.title: 1. Product Overview
docs.text:
Home · Next: Product Goals →

StudyFinder is a clinical research study discovery and outreach platform that helps users find relevant studies, organize opportunities, manage sponsor and provider contacts, and conduct outreach through automated email sequences.

The platform brings study data, contact information, scouting workflows, reporting, analytics, and outreach tools into one place so users can identify opportunities and act on them efficiently.

docs.title: What StudyFinder brings together
docs.text:
• Study data — study discovery across multiple sources (Study Discovery).
  • Contact information — sponsors, companies, PIs, and providers (Companies, Sponsors & Contacts).
  • Scouting workflows — surface and organize high-potential opportunities (Scouts).
  • Reporting & analytics — visibility into discovery and outreach activity (Tasks & Reports, Analytics).
  • Outreach tools — templates and automated email sequences (Email Sequences, Templates).

Access to these capabilities is governed by subscription tier.

Home · Next: Product Goals →

docs.title: 2. Product Goals
docs.text:
← Product Overview · Home · Next: Subscription Tiers →

StudyFinder aims to:

  • Help users discover relevant clinical studies quickly.
  • Make study search and filtering accurate, flexible, and easy to use.
  • Support efficient organization of promising opportunities through bookmarks and scouts.
  • Provide reliable sponsor, company, PI, and provider contact information.
  • Enable structured outreach through templates and email sequences.
  • Give users clear reporting and analytics on study discovery and outreach activity.
  • Ensure consistent, readable information across study cards and study detail pages.
  • Provide clear subscription-based access to features and usage limits.

← Product Overview · Home · Next: Subscription Tiers →

docs.title: 3. Subscription Tiers and Feature Access
docs.text:
← Product Goals · Home · Next: Core Product Areas →

StudyFinder offers four subscription tiers: Lite, Core, Growth, and Enterprise. Feature availability and usage limits vary by plan.

docs.title: Feature access matrix
docs.text: Feature Lite (Free) Core Growth Enterprise Users 1 user only; member invitations require an upgrade Unlimited Unlimited Unlimited Study Intelligence 1 therapeutic-area-only scout; 10 matches/week; no weekly report; All Studies and Companies unavailable Unlimited scouts; no limits; weekly scout reports; All Studies and Companies enabled Same as Core Same as Core Contact Intelligence Not available; upgrade required Full contact access Full contact access Full contact access TrialTrack CRM 1 Opportunity board only; Start-Up board, team management, and analytics unavailable Multiple Opportunity boards and standard analytics; team management and custom analytics require Enterprise Same as Core Full access, including team management and standard/custom analytics and reports AI FQ Filler Included Included Included Included AI Email Outreach Not available; upgrade required Not available; upgrade required Included Included

docs.title: 3.1 Lite Plan
docs.text:
The Lite plan is a free, limited-access option for users who want to explore StudyFinder's study discovery capabilities.

Includes: - One user - One therapeutic-area-only scout - Up to 10 study matches per week - AI FQ Filler - One Opportunity board in TrialTrack CRM

Limitations: - No weekly scout reports - No access to All Studies or Companies - No Contact Intelligence - No advanced TrialTrack CRM features - No analytics - No team management - No AI Email Outreach

docs.title: 3.2 Core Plan
docs.text:
Price: $199 per month

The Core plan provides full Study Intelligence and Contact Intelligence, with foundational TrialTrack CRM capabilities.

Includes: - Unlimited users - Unlimited scouts - Weekly scout reports - Access to All Studies and Companies - Full Contact Intelligence - Multiple Opportunity boards - Standard analytics and reporting - AI FQ Filler - TrialTrack CRM base functionality

Limitations: - AI Email Outreach is not included - Team management is not included - Custom analytics and reporting are not included

docs.title: 3.3 Growth Plan
docs.text:
Price: $299 per month

The Growth plan includes everything in Core and adds AI Email Outreach for automated sponsor and contact engagement.

Includes: - Everything in Core - AI Email Outreach - Unlimited users - Unlimited scouts - Weekly scout reports - Full Contact Intelligence - Standard analytics and reporting - AI FQ Filler

Limitations: - Team management is not included - Custom analytics and reporting are not included

docs.title: 3.4 Enterprise Plan
docs.text:
Price: $999 per month

The Enterprise plan is designed for organizations that require advanced CRM configuration, deeper reporting, and team-level management.

Includes: - Everything in Growth - Unlimited users - Unlimited scouts - Weekly scout reports - Full Contact Intelligence - Enterprise TrialTrack CRM configuration - Custom analytics and reporting - Team management and KPI tracking - AI FQ Filler - AI Email Outreach

docs.title: 3.5 Upgrade Paths
docs.text:
• Lite → Core: unlock full study access, company access, and Contact Intelligence.
  • Core → Growth: access AI Email Outreach.
  • Core / Growth → Enterprise: team management, custom analytics, custom reporting, and advanced CRM configuration.

← Product Goals · Home · Next: Core Product Areas →

docs.title: 4. Core Product Areas
docs.text:
← Subscription Tiers · Home

StudyFinder is organized into ten core product areas. Each has its own page with functionality and requirements.

# Area Purpose 4.1 Study Discovery Browse and manage studies across sources 4.2 Search & Filtering Narrow study results by relevant criteria 4.3 Study Cards & Study Details At-a-glance summaries and full study information 4.4 Scouts Review and organize high-potential opportunities 4.5 Companies, Sponsors & Contacts Contact intelligence for outreach 4.6 Email Sequences Automate multi-step outreach 4.7 Templates Reusable email content 4.8 Tasks & Reports Track work and summarize activity 4.9 Analytics Insight into discovery, scouting, and outreach 4.10 User Management Access and permission control

Feature availability across these areas is governed by subscription tier.

← Subscription Tiers · Home

docs.title: 4.9 Analytics
docs.text:
← Tasks & Reports · Home · Next: User Management →

Analytics provides insight into study discovery, scouting activity, contacts, and outreach performance.

docs.title: Requirements
docs.text:
• Users should be able to select date ranges through a clear date picker.
  • Analytics should accurately reflect the selected reporting period.
  • Metrics should help users understand study pipeline activity and outreach performance.
  • Results should be easy to interpret and consistent with underlying study and email sequence data.
  • Standard analytics should be available to Core, Growth, and Enterprise users.
  • Custom analytics and reporting should be available to Enterprise users.

See subscription tiers for access details.

← Tasks & Reports · Home · Next: User Management →

docs.title: 4.5 Companies, Sponsors, and Contacts
docs.text:
← Scouts · Home · Next: Email Sequences →

StudyFinder stores company, sponsor, PI, provider, and related contact information connected to study opportunities.

docs.title: Requirements
docs.text:
• Users should be able to search for companies, sponsors, PIs, and providers.
  • Contact search should return complete and relevant results.
  • Duplicate contacts should be minimized or clearly distinguished.
  • Similar PI or provider records should be reviewed carefully when names differ only by middle name or minor formatting.
  • Sponsor and company information should remain consistent across related study records.
  • Contact records should support outreach actions where permitted.
  • Contact Intelligence access should be restricted to eligible subscription tiers.

← Scouts · Home · Next: Email Sequences →

docs.title: 4.6 Email Sequences
docs.text:
← Companies, Sponsors & Contacts · Home · Next: Templates →

Email Sequences allow users to automate outreach to relevant contacts using reusable templates and scheduled steps.

docs.title: Core functionality
docs.text:
• Create multi-step email sequences.
  • Define delays between sequence steps.
  • Use templates for reusable outreach content.
  • Enroll contacts in sequences.
  • Track sequence progress and replies.
  • Stop or adjust automation when a recipient replies.

docs.title: Requirements
docs.text:
• Delay logic should be based on the previous sequence step.
  • Reply detection should prevent unnecessary follow-up messages.
  • Contacts added to a sequence should receive the appropriate starting step.
  • Sequence entry dates and labels should update correctly when schedules change.
  • Pagination should support more than 10 records reliably.
  • Attachments should display correctly regardless of text size.
  • Email composition should support signatures and logos.
  • Users should have access to a live preview before activating a sequence.
  • AI-assisted sequence drafting may be available on demand.
  • Digest email functionality may summarize outreach activity.
  • AI Email Outreach features should only be available to Growth and Enterprise users (subscription tiers).

← Companies, Sponsors & Contacts · Home · Next: Templates →

docs.title: 4.4 Scouts
docs.text:
← Study Cards & Study Details · Home · Next: Companies, Sponsors & Contacts →

Scouts support the review and organization of high-potential study opportunities.

Common report groupings include:

  • High Promise Finds
  • Rescue Radar
  • Other Findings

docs.title: Requirements
docs.text:
• Users should be able to identify, categorize, and track study opportunities.
  • Scout reports should support filtering and meaningful grouping of findings.
  • Reports should allow teams to prioritize studies for review or outreach.
  • Scout results should remain connected to the original study record where applicable.
  • Scout usage limits, match limits, and report availability should follow the user's subscription tier.

← Study Cards & Study Details · Home · Next: Companies, Sponsors & Contacts →

docs.title: 4.2 Search and Filtering
docs.text:
← Study Discovery · Home · Next: Study Cards & Study Details →

Users should be able to search and narrow study results using relevant criteria, including:

  • Therapeutic area
  • Study phase
  • Location
  • Posted date
  • Study status
  • Multicenter status
  • Academic or nonacademic status
  • Listed or not listed status
  • US-only availability

docs.title: Requirements
docs.text:
• Filters should return accurate and relevant study results.
  • Users should be able to combine multiple filters.
  • Date-related filters should clearly reflect the selected range.
  • Search results should remain consistent across the study listing and study detail views.
  • Negative or exclusion-based filtering may be supported where applicable.
  • Available filters may vary by subscription tier.

← Study Discovery · Home · Next: Study Cards & Study Details →

docs.title: 4.3 Study Cards and Study Details
docs.text:
← Search & Filtering · Home · Next: Scouts →

Study cards provide an at-a-glance summary of each study. Study Details provides the full information available for a selected study.

docs.title: Requirements
docs.text:
• Study cards should display titles, sponsor information, locations, and other core metadata clearly.
  • Study Details should show expanded information without text overlap or layout issues.
  • Sponsor names and titles should use consistent formatting across cards and detail pages.
  • Long study details should wrap or expand correctly without obscuring other fields.
  • Users should be able to bookmark studies from relevant views.
  • Access to studies and company details should follow subscription-tier permissions.

← Search & Filtering · Home · Next: Scouts →

docs.title: 4.1 Study Discovery
docs.text:
← Core Product Areas · Home · Next: Search & Filtering →

StudyFinder provides several areas for users to browse and manage studies:

  • For You — Personalized study recommendations.
  • All Studies — Complete study directory.
  • Bookmarks — Saved studies for later review.
  • ClinicalTrials.gov — Study records sourced from ClinicalTrials.gov.
  • ClinicalPulse — Additional study intelligence and opportunities.

Users should be able to open a study, review its details, identify relevant organizations or contacts, and save it for future action.

Access to All Studies and Companies is gated by subscription tier; Lite users have a therapeutic-area-only scout and a weekly match limit.

← Core Product Areas · Home · Next: Search & Filtering →

docs.title: 4.8 Tasks and Reports
docs.text:
← Templates · Home · Next: Analytics →

Tasks and Reports help users track outreach work and summarize study discovery activity.

docs.title: Requirements
docs.text:
• Users should be able to create and manage tasks related to studies, contacts, or outreach.
  • Reports should support relevant date ranges and filtering.
  • Business-day calculations should account for configured holidays where applicable.
  • Reporting should make it easy to identify completed work, active opportunities, and pending follow-up.
  • Advanced or custom reporting should be restricted to Enterprise users where applicable (subscription tiers).

← Templates · Home · Next: Analytics →

docs.title: 4.7 Templates
docs.text:
← Email Sequences · Home · Next: Tasks & Reports →

Templates allow users to save reusable email content for outreach and sequence creation.

docs.title: Requirements
docs.text:
• Users should be able to create, edit, duplicate, and organize templates.
  • Multi-step templates should preserve all entered data when new steps are added.
  • Template content should be reusable across email sequences.
  • Placeholder support should be clear and should only appear where applicable.

← Email Sequences · Home · Next: Tasks & Reports →

docs.title: 4.10 User Management
docs.text:
← Analytics · Home

User Management controls platform access and user-level permissions.

docs.title: Requirements
docs.text:
• Admins should be able to manage users, roles, and account access.
  • User search should return accurate results.
  • User lists should support alphabetical sorting.
  • Role-based permissions should control access to actions such as editing, creating, or managing studies.
  • Status changes should include confirmation prompts when appropriate.
  • Permissions should be consistently applied across StudyFinder features.
  • The number of users and invitation capabilities should follow the active subscription tier.

← Analytics · Home
